items.title: Improve mobile readability and shelf layout
actions: Reduce shelf/plank gap sizes to show more tapes on screen
Reduce dead space above and below the shelf
Make completed row labels smaller or collapsible so they don't block the view
Improve pinch-zoom to focus on the area being zoomed (not always center)

feedback.text: A bit hard to read - can pinch zoom but it's always central, and once a row has been completed it blocks view. You're using up room for the music stuff for example, and big gaps under shelf, and even above. Make more compact to show more of titles. Make shelf graphics and gaps smaller for more room for tapes.

items.title: Faster tape inspection / quick-browse mode
actions: Speed up the long-press-to-inspect flow — feels slow
Consider a quick-browse mode: tap left/right shelf edges to zoom into a 2x2 grid of that section
Make the X close on inspect view more responsive
Allow tap-to-fast-skip on character dialogue text

feedback.text: Hold on single title is a bit slow and you have to x close it, it's awkward - need something much faster to just get lots of information. Allow clicking on left or right of shelf to do a zoom in to show what's on there maybe? Showing 4 titles in a big zoomed in 2x2 grid or something while you hold?

items.title: Rethink customer interrupts — make them opt-in
actions: Change interrupts from auto-popup to player-initiated (e.g., 'Ask the Boss' button)
Add tap-to-skip for interrupt text animation
Don't offer hints for already-solved categories
Don't give away free hints — player should always choose to spend money
If keeping auto-interrupts, make them less frequent and non-blocking

feedback.text: Characters coming up is very distracting - if players want hint should just have a button so they can ask for one at the moment THEY become frustrated rather than intruding (maybe it's asking your nerdy boss or something) - it blocks when trying to select stuff. You also get a hint without paying for it. People want to try it themselves. If keeping this HAVE to allow tap to fast-skip text. Seems to offer hints for rows you've already guessed as well.

items.title: Weekly difficulty progression (Mon=easy → Sun=hard)
actions: Structure puzzles Mon–Sun with increasing difficulty like NYT Crossword
Monday: heavily themed, obvious categories (genre, franchise)
Friday+: mixed categories, overlapping movies, discovery-based
Show the week as Mon–Sun layout, not a rolling date strip
Let players see upcoming puzzle names to build anticipation
Themed days should be clearly labeled (puzzle title visible on game screen)

feedback.text: Day of week should complicate categories - so Mondays are always easy like type etc or have a very clearly described theme. Later are like starring, decade, director, etc, or mixing up categories where earlier days of week are themed and specifically called out by the name like you have now, which is easier and then gets harder through week being more of a jumble and having titles that overlap categories Ala connections. Week seems to have current date at the end - might be better to have it laid out Monday to Sunday and refresh each week so people feel progression and want to complete each day slot and feel that completion as well as their overall day streak. This can also help understand the clear ramp up of each day in terms of difficulty, like NYT crossword.

items.title: Show puzzle title/theme on the game screen
actions: Display the puzzle title next to the date in the HUD during gameplay
Include the day of week
This serves as a hint for themed puzzles and context for mixed ones

feedback.text: When on the game screen should have the day's title by the date too - to remind you of the hint / theme category if there is one or if it's a mix (maybe day of week as well). Right now it just shows New Arrivals and the date. It's Wednesday but the game today is Friday night Rush - should be themed around day of week consistently.

items.title: Pause before end screen — let the last solve breathe
actions: After solving the final category, pause on the completed shelf view
Show a 'View Results' or 'Next' button instead of auto-jumping to end screen
Give the player a moment to see all 4 categories revealed before stats

feedback.text: The game skips straight to end screen when completing - should wait at end and have a stats button or next button (where the pick 4 was) so you have a chance to read the last category and see the arrangements if you have no idea what it was. Even tho it has this on the next screen it feels like you want that moment with what you did before going to detailed stats.

items.title: Remove or minimize the radio/music UI
items.note: We have to include the 'Game Jam 2026' tag at the bottom, so we have a few pixels of space, and I really like the radio idea. Maybe I'll pick some better music/sounds, and make it clearer you can mute.
actions: Consider removing the KPEZ radio from the HUD — takes up space
If keeping, make it much smaller or hide-able
Music may not fit the core experience for all players

feedback.text: Should remove the music thing - takes up UI room and is distracting I think. And also doesn't fit with theme.

items.title: Fix menu navigation and 'Replay' label
actions: Return to main menu after finishing a game, not the splash page
Fix 'Replay' label for puzzles the player hasn't played yet
Clarify if replay lets you improve your score
Add a help button on the main menu for returning players

feedback.text: Back button after finishing a round should not return to start page, should return to main menu (to select another day). For a day I hadn't done before (Tuesday in this case) it says replay but I've never played it. Also what does replay allow? Can you improve score? Main menu should have a help button on it to show you how to play again (if you've said to skip from start page) - since can't go back to start and should always return to main menu.

items.title: Add external movie links (IMDb, Letterboxd, streaming)
actions: In the tape inspect view, add links to IMDb/Letterboxd/Rotten Tomatoes
Could show 'Where to Watch' streaming info
Encourages movie discovery beyond the game

feedback.text: I like that people can learn about movies tho - might be good to have the rotten tomatoes or IMDb or the where to stream link or letterboxd on the page so you can go find out more about movies.

items.title: Ensure clear difficulty tiers within each puzzle
actions: Make sure every puzzle has one easy, one medium, one hard, one devious category (like Connections)
The easy category should be 'stumble-upon-able' to help narrow down harder ones
Consider using IMDB review count as a proxy for movie recognizability

feedback.text: DO the categories have increasing difficulty within each puzzle like connections? Connections works by having an 'easier' category in the group of 4 categories so you can maybe stumble on that one which eliminates a lot of the crossover possibilities and helps you figure out the harder ones. Could probably gather that information from like # of reviews on IMDb or something.

items.title: AI-powered puzzle generation pipeline
actions: AI generates themed and mixed puzzles with proper difficulty tiers
Admin can proof and edit AI output before publishing
Backroom admin built with AI sparkle generation

feedback.text: Yeah if you could get some good AI directives (making categories that could be reused basically, and having it figure out how to make good combinations of a) themed days and b) no brainer, easy, medium, hard categories for each day like connections does, you could just end up having to proof it's work and have tons of weeks of content.
